members.name: ClearDocsFromScript
members.summary: Removes the documentation for the given [param script]. [b]Note:[/b] This should be called whenever the script is changed to keep the open documentation state up to date.

members.name: CloseFile
members.summary: Closes the file at the given [param path], discarding any unsaved changes. Returns [constant OK] on success or [constant ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND] if the file is not found.

members.name: GetCurrentEditor
members.summary: Returns the [ScriptEditorBase] object that the user is currently editing.

members.name: GetCurrentScript
members.summary: Returns a [Script] that is currently active in editor.

members.name: GetOpenScriptEditors
members.summary: Returns an array with all [ScriptEditorBase] objects which are currently open in editor.

members.name: GetOpenScripts
members.summary: Returns an array with all [Script] objects which are currently open in editor.

members.name: GetUnsavedFiles
members.summary: Returns an array of file paths of scripts with unsaved changes open in the editor.

members.name: GotoHelp
members.summary: Opens help for the given topic. The [param topic] is an encoded string that controls which class, method, constant, signal, annotation, property, or theme item should be focused. The supported [param topic] formats include [code]class_name:class[/code], [code]class_method:class:method[/code], [code]class_constant:class:constant[/code], [code]class_signal:class:signal[/code], [code]class_annotation:class:@annotation[/code], [code]class_property:class:property[/code], and [code]class_theme_item:class:item[/code], where [code]class[/code] is the class name, [code]method[/code] is the method name, [code]constant[/code] is the constant name, [code]signal[/code] is the signal name, [code]annotation[/code] is the annotation name, [code]property[/code] is the property name, and [code]item[/code] is the theme item. [codeblock] # Shows help for the Node class. class_name:Node # Shows help for the global min function. # Global objects are accessible in the `@GlobalScope` namespace, shown here. class_method:@GlobalScope:min # Shows help for get_viewport in the Node class. class_method:Node:get_viewport # Shows help for the Input constant MOUSE_BUTTON_MIDDLE. class_constant:Input:MOUSE_BUTTON_MIDDLE # Shows help for the BaseButton signal pressed. class_signal:BaseButton:pressed # Shows help for the CanvasItem property visible. class_property:CanvasItem:visible # Shows help for the GDScript annotation export. # Annotations should be prefixed with the `@` symbol in the descriptor, as shown here. class_annotation:@GDScript:@export # Shows help for the GraphNode theme item named panel_selected. class_theme_item:GraphNode:panel_selected [/codeblock]

members.name: GotoLine
members.summary: Goes to the specified line in the current script.

members.name: OpenScriptCreateDialog
members.signature: public void OpenScriptCreateDialog(String baseName, String basePath)
members.summary: Opens the script create dialog. The script will extend [param base_name]. The file extension can be omitted from [param base_path]. It will be added based on the selected scripting language.

members.name: RegisterSyntaxHighlighter
members.summary: Registers the [EditorSyntaxHighlighter] to the editor, the [EditorSyntaxHighlighter] will be available on all open scripts. [b]Note:[/b] Does not apply to scripts that are already opened.

members.name: ReloadOpenFiles
members.summary: Reloads all currently opened files. This should be used when opened files are changed outside of the script editor. The user may be prompted to resolve file conflicts, see [member EditorSettings.text_editor/behavior/files/auto_reload_scripts_on_external_change].

members.name: UnregisterSyntaxHighlighter
members.summary: Unregisters the [EditorSyntaxHighlighter] from the editor. [b]Note:[/b] The [EditorSyntaxHighlighter] will still be applied to scripts that are already opened.

members.name: UpdateDocsFromScript
members.summary: Updates the documentation for the given [param script]. [b]Note:[/b] This should be called whenever the script is changed to keep the open documentation state up to date.

signals.name: EditorScriptChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when user changed active script. Argument is a freshly activated [Script].

signals.name: ScriptClose
signals.summary: Emitted when editor is about to close the active script. Argument is a [Script] that is going to be closed.
